events.title: Ghana conference calls for formal apology for transatlantic slave trade
events.summary:
Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at event includes demand for compensation and debt relief

More than money: the logic of slavery reparations

A global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade.

Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity.

Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity.

Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering.

When more than 20 women accused a Colombian evangelical pastor in 2012 of sexually abusing them, the defendant’s lawyer sought to discredit the allegations by telling the court that they were “trepadoras” – a pejorative term meaning social climbers.

He ultimately secured his client’s acquittal – although the case remains under review by the supreme court – but footage of the remark resurfaced during Colombia’s presidential campaign, sparking outrage among many progressive voters.

events.title: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
events.summary:
Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majority

A Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” has defeated a leftwing senator by a razor-thin margin to win Colombia’s presidential runoff, in an election that promises to mark a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict.

With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, the far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than the leftist senator Iván Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A further 1.6% of ballots were cast blank.

events.title: Colombians vote in runoff election expected to trigger shift in decades-long armed conflict
events.summary:
Frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups

  • Ghost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff vote

Colombians are going to the polls in a presidential runoff expected to trigger a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict, now at its most violent point since the landmark 2016 peace agreement between the government and most of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc).

Polls show the frontrunner is the Trump-admiring far-right lawyer and millionaire businessman Abelardo de la Espriella, who has vowed to abandon President Gustavo Petro’s “total peace” plan of negotiating the disarmament of all criminal organisations and instead return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups.

events.title: Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive
events.summary:
Written in 1947, Kiyoshi Tanimoto’s account of the horrors of the atomic bomb attack will be published in August and is being made into a film

The memoir of a man who survived the horrors of Hiroshima is to be published for the first time this summer after its discovery in a US archive.

The 230-page memoir was written almost 80 years ago by Kiyoshi Tanimoto, who witnessed the city’s destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped in 1945. He will now be portrayed in a major feature film by Takehiro Hira, whose acclaimed roles include the detective in the Netflix Japanese-British drama Giri/Haji. Pre-production begins in November, ahead of the shoot in February 2027.

About 12,500 people – men who had served in the Royal Dutch East Indies and their families – came from a group of Indonesian islands to the Netherlands in 1951, many having been given no choice. They thought it would be a temporary evacuation after Indonesia had won independence.

The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with 23mm reported in Kranj.

The Victoria Cross recipients have been invited to this, which is appropriate, I’m comfortable about that. What will be opened – the Australian War Memorial, it’s an utterly fantastic extension to what is, I think, the most sacred building in our country. And we are very much looking forward to that. The prime minister will be there. It’s a very significant moment for the nation.

If we look over the longer term, and people buy houses over the longer term, we will see housing prices continue to grow. I suppose one of our concerns is that perhaps the changes don’t go far enough in addressing the rorts and the criminal behaviour that we’ve seen embedded in the NDIS system now. We want to make sure that they are appropriately addressed.

And we also want to make sure that Australia’s most vulnerable are well cared for. And there does seem to be some oversights in there. So I have no problems with scrutiny of legislation.

events.title: Andy Burnham prepares for power as emotional Keir Starmer bows out

New Makerfield MP could get keys to No 10 unopposed after British prime minister’s resignation paves way for successor

Keir Starmer has finally bowed to intense pressure to stand down as British prime minister as he conceded that he was no longer the right man to lead the country, leaving Andy Burnham all but certain to succeed him.

In an extraordinary day at Westminster, Starmer announced a timetable for his departure after months of growing discontent among Labour MPs and cabinet ministers, many unnerved by the threat from Reform UK before the next general election.

Burnham will begin to set out his policies next week with a series of speeches to demonstrate a symbolic shift from Starmer’s government, starting with the economy and devolution.

He is considering appointing Ed Miliband as chancellor in order to challenge Treasury orthodoxy but has not made a final decision. Sources said Burnham was aware of the potential risks with business and the unions opposed to the move, but could be prepared to make the argument.

Shabana Mahmood is expected to stay at the Home Office after the former Greater Manchester mayor praised the home secretary for “facing up” to the big issues on immigration during the byelection campaign.

Wes Streeting could be appointed to one of the top cabinet jobs, but did “not come with any leverage” to discussions, as campaign sources rejected his claims he had the numbers to run. Others have argued for him to be appointed chancellor to reassure the markets.

Starmer loyalists are still seeking a candidate who could stand against Burnham – depending on whether Miliband was chancellor. Darren Jones has been touted as a possibility, and although sources said he was not organising a run, they stopped short of a categorical denial.

The Senate on Monday passed a bipartisan measure aimed at lowering housing costs by streamlining construction and permitting, ending months of fraught negotiations on a priority for both parties ahead of November’s midterm elections.

The 21st Century Road to Housing Act would limit investors’ ability to buy homes, waive some federal permitting rules in a bid to ease new construction, and authorize pilot programs to facilitate grants for home improvements and planning affordable housing. It passed the Senate overwhelmingly, with a vote of 85-5, and now heads to the House of Representatives.

events.summary: Indian women fight back against toxic coal dust, the US hits a solar milestone, and there's good news for soil in Kenya and cars worldwide.

events.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
events.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

events.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee
events.summary: The Czech government has approved a bill to put the funding of public service media under direct political control. Critics warn that if passed, the bill would put a pillar of Czech democracy at risk.

events.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
events.summary: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

events.title: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad built on African roots
events.summary: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad has leaders with African heritage. The diversity of the team is a strength.

events.title: Baerbock: 'Germany team is an example to Germany's kids'
events.summary: The president of the UN General Assembly, Annalena Baerbock, praises the diversity of Germany's football team as it plays in the World Cup.

events.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
events.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz: German military prepares for possible deployment
events.summary: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

events.title: Israel says it's cutting ties to EU's Kaja Kallas
events.summary: Israel's foreign minister says he is severing contact with the EU's foreign affairs chief Kaja Kallas. Gideon Saar attributed the move to a media report alleging that Kallas compared Israel to apartheid-era South Africa.

events.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
events.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

events.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
events.summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

events.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
events.summary: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

events.title: Nathaniel Brown: The left back Germany have been waiting for
events.summary: A strong option at left back is overdue for Germany. Nathaniel Brown is quickly becoming a star at the World Cup.

events.title: How a Belarusian exile vanished in waters held by Russia
events.summary: Former sports official Anatol Kotau was last seen boarding a private yacht in Turkey in 2025. DW and its partners investigated his journey to and disappearance in Russia-controlled "gray zone" waters in the Black Sea.

events.title: Germany: Reports of antisemitism in 2025 remain high
events.summary: The Federal Association of Departments for Research and Information on Antisemitism (RIAS) recorded over 8,700 cases they classify as antisemitic hostility in 2025, many of which were connected with the State of Israel.

events.title: CRISPR gene-editing for crops: Precision tool or new risk?
events.summary: The EU has eased restrictions on crops developed using novel genetic techniques. While supporters say the technology could help farmers adapt to climate change, critics argue its effects remain poorly understood.

events.title: Ebola and the US patient spotlight global health 'injustice'
events.summary: The story of the US doctor who survived Ebola with experimental treatment in Germany highlights wider inequities in global health. It also reveals how sharply medical standards can differ between African countries.

events.title: Is Russia heading for a fuel crisis?
events.summary: Russia's fuel output is falling after Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries. Seasonal spikes in gasoline and diesel demand could push the system into a deeper crisis, experts say.

events.title: Why the Williams sisters and other stars are playing longer
events.summary: More than three decades after they burst onto the tennis scene, Venus and Serena Williams are still playing at the top level. Why are we seeing elite athletes carrying on into their 40s and beyond?

events.title: Bavarian top court rules state spy agency can monitor AfD
events.summary: Bavaria's regional domestic intelligence agency can monitor the right-wing populist AfD, the state's top court has ruled. Several states are trying to implement similar steps, with the opposition party challenging it.

events.title: World Cup dreams come true for young German footballers
events.summary: Three young US college soccer players from Germany have had the opportunity to meet some of their World Cup heroes, as the men's natonal team's base camp is just around the corner.

events.title: Germany's Merz: Who are his international allies?
events.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has forged relations with Donald Trump, Emmanuel Macron, Keir Starmer and Giorgia Meloni. With some he is surprisingly close.

events.title: Failed joint jet project highlights Europe's defense dilemma
events.summary: The collapse of a Franco-German fighter jet project and a faltering joint battle tank program show how national industrial interests can outweigh common defense objectives.

events.title: Germany: 25 years since Nazi forced labor compensation began
events.summary: A German fund set up to compensate the millions forced to work for the Nazi regime is marking 25 years since the first payments were made. But for many victims, it was much too late.

events.title: How Germany and Poland rebooted relations 35 years ago
events.summary: When Germany and Poland signed the Treaty on Good Neighborliness, Friendship and Cooperation in June 1991, they chose partnership over enmity and became close NATO and EU allies. But it hasn't all been plain sailing.

events.title: European Parliament approves US trade deal to lower tariffs
events.summary: The European Parliament has approved cutting tariffs on many US imports to comply with a deal signed last year. US President Trump had given the bloc a July 4 deadline to meet its side of the deal.

events.title: Germany news: Population shrinks for first time since 2020
events.summary: A dip in net migration meant it could not cancel out Germany's low birth rates in 2025. Meanwhile, fuel prices fell amid news of a possible deal between the US and Iran. And Merz gives Trump a Germany jersey. More on DW.

events.title: World Cup: Cape Verde hero and Iran suffer US travel woes
events.summary: After starring in Cape Verde's draw with Spain, goalkeeper Vozinha shared his sadness that an expensive travel bond stopped his mother from attending. On the same day, Iran's coach said his team were being "oppressed."

events.title: Leipzig Bach festival: Encouraging dialog
events.summary: Talking, debating and listening are skills we can all learn, particularly in the light of current global conflicts. The Leipzig Bachfest shows how Johann Sebastian Bach's music can serve as a model here.

events.title: Artist JR's wrapped bridge opens in Paris in nod to Christo
events.summary: After a weather-delayed start, the French street artist's cavernous optical illusion takes over one of the most iconic bridges in Paris. It's a tribute to Christo and Jeanne-Claude's famous "Pont Neuf Wrapped."

events.title: Musk to sue German broadcaster ZDF over 'hunt for migrants'
events.summary: Elon Musk said he was taking legal action against one of Germany's public broadcasters after it reported that he had called for "a hunt for migrants" in Belfast.

events.title: EU, Ukraine start formal accession talks after Orban delay
events.summary: The EU has formally opened the first stage of accession negotiations with Ukraine. This comes two years later than planned, as former Hungarian PM Viktor Orban was blocking the process prior to his election defeat.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening won't end shipping risks
events.summary: A proposed US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is raising hopes for global shipping and oil markets. But naval mines, high insurance costs and geopolitical risks mean disruption could persist for months.

events.title: Ibrahim Maza: Algeria's 'multicultural kid' from Berlin
events.summary: Ibo Maza had his choice of three different countries. Instead of his native Germany, the Bayer Leverkusen forward is representing Algeria, the country of his father, at the World Cup in North America.

events.title: US-Iran deal: When will oil prices fall?
events.summary: A potential US-Iran deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz could ease the global energy crunch, but oil prices and supplies may take months to stabilize as shipping restarts and infrastructure recovers.

events.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
events.summary: There are still mines in the Straight of Hormuz, delaying the opening for commercial shipping. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?

events.title: Russia strikes leave historic Kyiv cathedral in flames
events.summary: The Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was set on fire as Russia struck multiple cities in Ukraine overnight. Meanwhile, Moscow reported three deaths from a Ukrainian drone attack.

events.title: Manuel Neuer: The aura of a record-breaking goalkeeper
events.summary: Germany's hopes at the 2026 World Cup are connected to world-class goalkeeper Manuel Neuer's fitness and form.

events.title: Germany start 2026 World Cup with win over Curacao
events.summary: For the first time since they won the tournament in 2014, Germany won their opening game at the World Cup.

events.title: Swiss voters reject population cap proposal
events.summary: A proposal to introduce a population cap of 10 million in the country has failed. It was put forward by a hard-right party.

events.title: Manuel Neuer fit to join Germany in first World Cup match
events.summary: Veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer is set to play in Germany's first match in this year's World Cup. He had retired from the national team in August 2024.

events.title: The creatives trying to fix Germany's bureaucracy
events.summary: Berlin played host to the 9th annual Creative Bureaucracy Festival this week, where highly-motivated public service workers explained why a healthy democracy needs a functioning state.

events.title: Germany news: President Steinmeier heads to Asia
events.summary: German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier is kicking off a trip to Asia. And veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer will take to the field with the national squad in Germany's first match of the 2026 World Cup.

events.title: Fact check: These FIFA World Cup ticket sites are fake
events.summary: Desperate to get hold of tickets for the football World Cup, many fans face being scammed by sites set up to sell fake tickets. Here's how to make sure you don't fall into the trap.

events.title: Gold rush atmosphere at Berlin's military air trade show
events.summary: This year's ILA Berlin Air Show is breaking records, primarily because of the booming defense sector, which is reaping billions in contracts as a result of wars and crises.

events.title: Germany faces EU penalty over gender pay gap directive
events.summary: Germany was required to incorporate the EU Pay Transparency Directive into its national law, but it has missed the deadline.

events.title: Why India wants German submarines – and what Pakistan and China have to do with it
events.summary: A multibillion-dollar submarine deal between India and Germany reflects New Delhi's push for military self-reliance — and Berlin's growing interest in the Indo-Pacific.

events.title: Swiss to vote on whether to cap population at 10 million
events.summary: On June 14, Swiss voters will be able to decide on a far-right initiative to curb future immigration. What will it mean for the economy — and the country's European neighbors — if they approve the proposal?

events.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
events.summary: SpaceX floated on the Nasdaq stock exchange on Friday, with shares trading 25% above the opening price at one point. The IPO has made Musk the world's first trillionaire, at least on paper.

events.title: German students up in arms about funding cuts
events.summary: Germany's research minister, Dorothee Bär, says the situation of German students is "very privileged" and has rejected reform of the Federal Training Assistance Act (BAföG), thereby turning many students against her.

events.title: Fast-track border checks, return centers, 'mandatory solidarity': What the EU’s new asylum rules could change — and what they may not.
events.summary: Germany aims to further reduce the number of new asylum seekers. The reformed EU asylum law (CEAS) came into effect on Friday, but experts say it is unlikely to achieve all it sets out to do.

events.title: British artist David Hockney dies at 88
events.summary: Considered a superstar of pop art, the UK painter was one of the world's most influential artists during his lifetime.

events.title: Jonathan Tah's first World Cup and the long wait
events.summary: The 2026 World Cup will be the Bayern Munich defender's first for Germany. This moment has been a long time coming.

events.title: Julian Nagelsmann: The coach struggling to speak to Germany
events.summary: Considered German football's X factor during Euro 2024, head coach Julian Nagelsmann enters the 2026 World Cup with more questions than optimism.

events.title: Lab-grown Tyrannosaurus leather bag fails to sell at auction
events.summary: The handbag promoted as the "world's first T. rex leather product" didn't find a buyer at auction. Is it more chicken than dinosaur?

events.title: Preaching and teaching in a warming world
events.summary: US pastors, a Ugandan musician and salmon-friendly tech delivered good environmental news this week.

events.title: World Cup kicks off in Mexico City
events.summary: Mexico won against South Africa after the opening ceremony in Mexico City which featured Colombian star Shakira and Nigerian singer Burna Boy. Football fans are set for nearly 40 days of action across three countries.

events.title: Is there anything GLP-1 agonist drugs cannot do?
events.summary: Every other month, researchers find new uses for GLP-1 agonist drugs, like Mounjaro and Ozempic — now even as a treatment for substance abuse. It's classic drug repurposing, a technique that saved lives during COVID.

events.title: Musk's SpaceX IPO holds both promise and peril
events.summary: SpaceX, Elon Musk's rocket and AI company, presents potential for investors. But its futuristic, unproven plans also carry significant financial risks.

events.title: Sex doll art sparks debate at German president's residence
events.summary: Beyond a headline-grabbing bronze, contemporary artworks draw attention to political power at an exhibition held at Bellevue Palace before the state building closes for renovation.

events.title: World Cup 2026: How an old song from Bosnia went viral
events.summary: Some have already called it the unofficial song of the 2026 World Cup. So how did a tune that was more than a decade old take the Balkans — and beyond — by storm?

events.title: Neo-Nazi lost an east German election, but extremism remains
events.summary: A neo-Nazi candidate surprisingly lost a mayoral election by a small margin in the eastern state of Saxony. Many are relieved. But one win doesn't make the extremism problem go away.

events.title: 'Disclosure Day': For Spielberg, aliens are not just fiction
events.summary: As Steven Spielberg releases a new film on extraterrestrials, here's a brief history of modern alien conspiracies and why scientists are now taking UFO reports seriously.

events.title: World Cup: Edin Dzeko still strong for Bosnia-Herzegovina
events.summary: With so much unpleasantness surrounding this year's World Cup it's easy to forget that there's anything about it that can elicit a smile. But the story of Bosnia-Herzegovina captain Edin Dzeko sure can.

events.title: The climate conversation many US pastors avoid
events.summary: As the Trump administration undoes scores of environmental protections, some Christian leaders are inviting their congregations to do as the Bible asks and be good stewards of the land. But many others stay silent.

events.title: Leipzig Bachfest 2026: Bach's music as hit parade
events.summary: The Leipzig Bach Festival honors the composer with a hit parade. Over 8,000 fans from 20 countries cast their votes. But Bach is celebrated in many other countries around the world as well.

events.title: Germany upbeat as final World Cup preparations begin
events.summary: As the German men's national football team embark on their final preparations for the World Cup at their base camp in North Carolina, they're looking to make amends for disappointing tournaments in Russia and Qatar.

events.title: Why Anthropic has the edge over OpenAI in IPO race
events.summary: The rivalry between Sam Altman and Dario Amodei is entering a new phase. Both AI bosses are planning public listings and hope to use the money to outpace each other in the race to build superintelligent AI.

events.title: Will Ebola have an impact on the World Cup?
events.summary: While ticket prices, heat and politics have made headlines, Ebola has been a World Cup worry for DR Congo. The African nation has qualified for the first time in 52 years, but does the outbreak have wider implications?

events.title: Somali referee denied US entry, dropped from FIFA World Cup 2026
events.summary: Omar Artan, Africa's top referee in 2025, was set to become the first Somali national to officiate at a FIFA World Cup before being denied entry by US authorities

events.title: Will Mexico's World Cup party be spoiled by teacher protest?
events.summary: Just before the World Cup kicks off, host country Mexico faces a political tug-of-war. Striking teachers have occupied the central fan zone and have pledged to continue protesting.

events.title: Depression in romantic relationships: You, me and the illness
events.summary: Depression can be a huge challenge for a relationship. Loved ones of someone with depression often feel helpless. But there is actually a lot they can do ― and a lot they shouldn't.

events.title: Jafar Panahi's prison sentence confirmed by Iranian court
events.summary: Following a retrial for a verdict that had been reached while the prominent Iranian director was abroad, the Revolutionary Court in Tehran has upheld a one-year jail sentence.

events.title: High-functioning depression: When success masks suffering
events.summary: People with depression don't always feel overwhelmed or struggle to get things done — some are highly efficient and productive, despite their low moods. So it can't be that bad, right? Wrong!

events.title: Why businesses are offering 'Quiet Hour'
events.summary: More peace and quiet, less stress — the Quiet Hour is an initiative for people with invisible disabilities. An increasing number of stores and institutions in Germany are launching similar campaigns.

events.title: Desalination hopes and reformed poachers
events.summary: EVs surge in Ethiopia, former poachers offer seahorse tours and India's ghost villages are repopulated.

events.title: SSRI antidepressants are often 'carelessly prescribed'
events.summary: SSRIs, like Prozac and Zoloft, are among the world's most-prescribed psychiatric drugs. These antidepressants help a lot of people. But they don't always work. In fact, some experts suggest they are overprescribed.

events.title: Why Yad Vashem is coming to Germany
events.summary: "Remember" — every child in Israel knows the meaning of this Jewish commandment: Never forget the Holocaust. Now Yad Vashem wants to make sure people in Germany remember it too.

events.title: Germans at Russia's SPIEF: Who's really there?
events.summary: Russian officials and media claim German businesses are comming back to the St. Petersburg Economic Forum despite the war in Ukraine. But who is actually attending Russia's flagship event this year?

events.title: Can Hawaii wean itself off oil imports?
events.summary: Hawaii imports much of its fuel — and pays the price. From solar to geothermal, the state is searching for a way out of fossil fuel dependence.

events.title: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
events.summary: The Iranian-born author of the acclaimed graphic novel and film offered a deeply personal account of Iran's Islamic Revolution.

events.title: Iran war: Even a peace deal won't fix energy crunch
events.summary: The growing energy and supply chain crisis is unlikely to ease, even if the US and Iran step back from the brink. Experts warn that reopening the Strait of Hormuz and repairing infrastructure could take months or years.

events.title: Wim Wenders withdraws film with disputed child nude scene
events.summary: Actress Nastassja Kinski spent years calling for the removal of a sexualized scene from Wim Wenders' film "The Wrong Move," in which she appeared topless at the age of 13.

events.title: Google mosquito army: Scientists say 'we must take action'
events.summary: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

events.title: Backrooms: The creepy internet phenomenon, explained
events.summary: A low-budget horror film directed by a 20-year-old YouTuber is beating the new Star Wars movie at the box office. Behind its success is an entire viral online maze.

events.title: Saudi Arabia and Russia draw closer amid OPEC upheaval
events.summary: As Saudi Arabia and Russia deepen cooperation, their combined influence over global oil supply is growing. Could this shift the balance of power in energy markets?

events.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?
events.summary: Billions of euros sit untouched in dormant bank accounts in Germany. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.

events.title: Legendary warrior women: The history of the Amazons
events.summary: They're said to have cut off their breasts to better draw their bows and even to have killed their male offspring. What is the history behind the myths surrounding the Amazons?

events.title: Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
events.summary: Electric vehicles have taken off in Ethiopia. Key to the shift: a world-first ban on importing fossil fuel-powered vehicles.

events.title: Mozart the idol: Searching for beauty in times of crisis
events.summary: Mozart's music evokes lightness and beauty. In times of war and destruction, people often invoke the ideals of the classical era. But is this just an escape from reality? The Mozartfest Würzburg offers different answers.

events.title: How China keeps North Korea's economy alive
events.summary: Xi Jinping is visiting North Korea for the first time since 2019. Pyongyang relies on China for 95% of its legitimate trade; hacking, arms deals and remittances also help it stay afloat.

events.title: German town prints its own cash to cut CO2 emissions
events.summary: It started as a school project to promote local businesses in a remote Bavarian region. Now, the "Chiemgauer" currency helps to cut emissions.

events.title: DNA had one rule. Bacteria didn't get the memo
events.summary: Every cell in every organism on Earth copies DNA the same way. Except one bacterial protein — quietly doing something scientists had never seen before.

events.title: Space race: Why Portugal is reaching for the stars
events.summary: A spaceport is being built on a small Portuguese island in the Atlantic. Is Portugal on the verge of becoming a space nation?

events.title: Swing Youth: In Nazi Germany, jazz was an act of defiance
events.summary: The Nazis denounced jazz music as "degenerate art," despite its widespread popularity in Germany. As the Nazis clamped down on expression, groups of jazz-loving teenagers formed the Swing Youth to rebel.

events.title: Goodbye, Norma Jeane: How Marilyn Monroe changed Hollywood
events.summary: Long seen primarily as a sex symbol, Marilyn Monroe is now viewed as an early advocate for women in Hollywood. On the 100th anniversary of her birth, DW looks at the star who balanced fame and feminist agency.

events.title: International aid cuts complicate Congo's Ebola epidemic
events.summary: The scaling back of development aid has contributed to the inability to contain the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. German organizations are calling for a major spending boost to help stop the spread.

events.title: Pigeons' livers may enable magnetic navigation when the sun doesn't shine, study finds
events.summary: A research team in Germany has published new data on how pigeons use magnetic fields as a navigation method when visibility is limited. A key part of the long-sought answer could lie in an unexpected organ, their livers.

events.title: The Superbug spill: When medicine goes rogue
events.summary: Pharmaceuticals have been found in waterways almost everywhere in the world, fueling antibiotic-resistant superbugs that no longer respond to treatment. Is there a way out?

events.title: Suez, Panama canals charge for transit — why can't Hormuz?
events.summary: Iran has been widely condemned for demanding up to $2 million for vessels to ship through the Strait of Hormuz. DW explores the reasons why Egypt can charge for the Suez Canal and Panama for its waterway, but Iran can't.

events.title: New Ebola cases in Congo: What you need to know
events.summary: The virus spreads from direct person-to-person contact. But here's what makes it especially lethal: it persists in corpses, and funeral practices often take place precisely when bodies are most infectious.

events.title: Iran war: Oil shortages threaten global energy security
events.summary: Strategic petroleum reserves are declining rapidly as countries release emergency crude supplies to cushion the economic impact of the Middle East conflict.

events.title: Europe's energy problem isn't green power — it's storage
events.summary: Solar and wind produce lots of energy — but not always at the right time. More battery storage could help Europe to stabilize prices and replace polluting fossil fuel energy, but roadblocks remain.

events.title: Germany eyes heat pumps as Iran war drives energy costs up
events.summary: The war in the Middle East is threatening fossil fuel supplies and causing energy prices to rise in Germany. Could that drive a surge in demand for heat pumps?

events.title: Ferrari unveils its first all-electric car, the 4-door Luce
events.summary: The Italian supercar giant has released images of what will be both its first ever pure EV, and its first car that can seat five people. The Luce eyes a younger clientele and the increasingly competitive Chinese market.

events.title: The wind boom Trump couldn't stop
events.summary: Even with fierce opposition, offshore wind projects are multiplying along US coasts — signaling a massive shift in energy.

events.title: Blinded by the light pollution: Cities seek to restore night
events.summary: Artificial lights are blotting out the moon and stars, harming human health, and disrupting the natural world. Why is this a problem? And how can we stop it without sitting in the dark?

events.title: Romania parliament rejects liberal PM-designate Adrian Vestea
events.summary: Romania's parliament rejected prime minister-designate Adrian Vestea on Monday, extending months of political turmoil in the EU and NATO member bordering Ukraine and raising the prospect of a snap election if a second government nominee also fails to secure lawmakers' backing.

events.title: World Cup 2026: France-Iraq match suspended as stadium is evacuated due to thunderstorms
events.summary: Heavy rain and an approaching thunderstorm delayed the second half of France's World Cup match against Iraq on Monday, forcing thousands of fans to seek shelter in the first weather-related disruption of the tournament. The stoppage came as severe weather also threatened another fixture in nearby New Jersey.

events.title: Vance says UN nuclear inspectors will return to Iran as US suspends sanctions
events.summary: The United States temporarily eased sanctions on Iranian oil on Monday after Vice President JD Vance said Tehran had agreed to allow UN nuclear inspectors back into the country, marking a significant step in renewed negotiations aimed at curbing Iran's nuclear programme and stabilising the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Rubio heads to UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain to discuss Iran deal with Gulf allies
events.summary: Starting Tuesday, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is slated to visit three Gulf allies that were attacked by Iran during the US and Israeli war: the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Bahrain. Rubio appears to have his work cut out for him. The US’s Gulf allies were not warned in advance of “Operation Epic Fury” and now, many of them are being asked to invest in Iran’s reconstruction. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.

events.title: Ethiopian PM's party secures win in national election
events.summary: In tonight's edition, Ethiopia's ruling Prosperity Party takes roughly 90% of seats in elections that hand Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed another half decade in power. Also, in Tunisia, inflation topped 5 percent last month causing price hikes in food and essentials across the board. And Cape Verde keeps on coming after its two-all draw against two-time World Cup champions Uruguay.

events.title: ‘Messi is the enemy of Allah’: World Cup hoax falsely blames Algerian fans for chant
events.summary: Lionel Messi is now the all-time top scorer in World Cup history, after scoring twice Monday against Austria in the 2026 World Cup. His first match, where he scored a hat-trick against Algeria, saw some online controversy. According to internet users, Algerian fans allegedly targeted him with an aggressive chant, calling Messi the "enemy of Allah" and threatening Argentina. But the audio is manipulated, and an old Arab protest chant against al-Sisi.

events.title: Turnover at 10 Downing Street raises questions about 'whether Britain is indeed governable'
events.summary: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigned on Monday, paving the way for Britain to have its seventh prime minister in just over a decade. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Martin Farr, Senior Lecturer in Contemporary British History at Newcastle University, says that the frequency of changes at 10 Downing Street since the Brexit vote "is very concerning and we're having many more questions about whether Britain is indeed governable".

events.title: World Cup 2026: Messi makes history as Argentina dominate Austria
World Cup 2026: Messi makes history as Argentina dominate Austria
events.summary: Argentina defeated Austria 2-0 in Arlington, Texas, with Lionel Messi scoring twice to become the all-time leading goal-scorer in World Cup history. The 38-year-old Argentine legend broke Miroslav Klose's record of 16 goals, cementing his status as one of football's greatest players. With two victories from two matches, Argentina have mathematically secured their passage to the last 32.

events.title: Shooting in Montreal Jewish neighbourhood leaves three dead, including suspect
events.summary: A shooting in a Jewish neighbourhood in Montreal on Monday left three people dead, including the suspected shooter, local police said. A police officer and an unidentified resident were also killed.

events.title: Will Colombia's right-wing hardliner De la Espriella be able to govern?
events.summary: Colombia on Sunday elected 47-year-old hardliner and millionaire lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella as the country’s next president just four years after electing Gustavo Petro the first left-wing president in the country's history. De la Espriella won the majority vote and received support from right-wingers in the US – but given his outsider status, will he be able to enact his policies?

events.title: France - Iraq live: Mbappé doubles as match resumes after stadium evacuation
events.summary: France face Iraq in a crucial Group I clash at Philadelphia's stadium, but severe thunderstorms could potentially force a postponement of kick-off. The French, fresh from a commanding 3-1 victory over Senegal, can secure their place in the last 32 with another win. Follow our live coverage.

events.title: Ukrainian attacks prompt Russian-held Crimea to halt civilian gasoline sales
events.summary: Ukraine has intensified its drone attacks on the Russian-controlled territory of Crimea, prompting a fuel shortage on the peninsula. The campaign has prompted officials there to halt gasoline sales to the public and has caused a larger threat to Russia's fleet in the Black Sea. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis has the story.

events.title: Labour's last chance? Burnham set to replace Starmer as UK faces far-right surge
events.summary: Another post-Brexit prime minister bites the dust. Keir Starmer owning up to the obvious, that he's too unpopular to lead the Labour Party into the next general election. After replacing the hard left's Jeremy Corbyn, the same Starmer won a whopping landslide two years ago on the promise of stability after all the chaos of Tory rule. Where did it all go wrong? Can the heavy favorite to replace him win back disgruntled Labour voters who first flocked to the Conservatives and now to the far-right Reform party?

events.title: US-Iran peace deal 'won't even come close to the expansive detailed Obama agreement'
events.summary: Nadia Massih welcomes Larry Sabato, Director of the University of Virginia Center for Politics. JD Vance is hailing "good progress" on US-Iran peace talks, following President Trump's threats to hit Iran "harder than ever before". Sabato reminds us that this war is "almost universally seen as a fiasco" in the US. Seventy percent of Americans are against the war, which is unprecedented. Sabato also offers a scathing assessment of Trump's ever-changing war strategy: "The president cannot stick to a script, and he cannot stick to a prearranged strategy". And so he warns that "it's going to be a long 60 days!"

events.title: ‘The dad is useless’: Anger grows over French journalist’s Doku criticism
events.summary: When Belgium star Jeremy Doku said he wanted to leave the World Cup to attend the birth of his son, it drew a furious reaction from French sports TV presenter France Pierron, who labelled childbirth “disgusting” and said fathers being there was “completely useless”. Her comments have since provoked outrage on social media and elicited support for Doku from fellow footballers.

events.title: As Cuba's power crisis deepens, Havana residents speak out
events.summary: Cubans are in the grips of a deepening power and fuel crisis – exacerbated by a US oil blockade launched earlier this year – that has left the country's poorest struggling to cope. FRANCE 24's exclusive report found that residents in Havana are now turning against their own government.

events.title: Afro B celebrates his Ivorian roots on 'Mapouka'
events.summary: In this edition of our arts24 music show, Jennifer Ben Brahim chats with British Afrobeats superstar Afro B. He is the brains behind the smash hit "Drogba (Joanna)", one of first Afrobeats songs to break the US charts. He has collaborated over the years with the likes of French Montana and Chris Brown. He is back with a new single called "Mapouka", a love letter to Ivory Coast. It’s the first taste of his upcoming album, "Afro Ivoire".

events.title: French court refuses trial on use of toxic insecticide in French West Indies
events.summary: A French court has ruled against a trial on the use of a known toxic insecticide in the French West Indies, shutting down the possibility of an investigation in a health scandal that has been ongoing for 20 years. Despite being labelled as possibly carcinogenic in 1979, chlordécone was used between 1972 until 1993 - thanks to a by-law that allowed it to circumvent its ban, introduced in 1990. FRANCE 24's Shirli Sitbon tells us more.

events.title: US hails 'good progress' with Iran after first round of talks concludes
events.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has said that a first round of US-Iran talks in Switzerland had laid a good foundation for reaching a final deal on ending the Middle East war that the US and Israel began in February. Vance's comments came after he met with Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf on June 21 and 22. The mediation effort in Switzerland had rocky moments, but the talks also led to some agreements between the two sides.

events.title: How can France adapt to recurrent heatwaves?
events.summary: France is seeing a second consecutive heatwave in less than a month. Half the country is now under red alert as temperatures are expected to rise to 43°C in the southwestern city of Bordeaux and 39°C in the capital Paris. According to weather service Méteo-France, out of 51 heatwaves recorded nationwide since 1947, two-thirds have occurred since 2000 and about half of those episodes in the last 15 years.

events.title: A look back at Starmer's two years in power following his resignation
events.summary: Britain's Keir Starmer has announced he's stepping down. The prime minister forced to resign after last month's loses in local elections. Starmer is expected to stay in the role until a new Labour party leader is picked. He is now set to become the country's shortest-serving Labour PM in history.

events.title: France aims for second win in World Cup group stage
events.summary: France is playing Iraq on June 22 for the second round of the groupe stage of the 2026 World Cup. The game is taking place in Philadelphia, and 'Les Bleus' will be looking to get a second win after their victory against Senegal. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes tells us more from Philadelphia's iconic Rocky statue.

events.title: Burnham confirms Starmer succession bid as he is sworn in as MP
events.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigned on June 22, forced out by his own party after missteps and mistakes soured voters’ goodwill following a landslide election in 2024. His likely successor, Andy Burnham, has just been sworn in as an MP and has no official rival to run for party leader. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle takes a look.

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Warnings of ‘dangerous cycle of escalation’ in Ukraine

events.title: Myanmar: Aid decline compounds suffering amid ongoing military attacks

events.title: Afghanistan in crisis: Drought, malnutrition and a worsening humanitarian situation
events.summary: Imagine being one of a family of nine and sitting down to a meal of potato peelings and other scraps, boiled up into a soup. This is the harsh reality for many of Afghanistan’s most vulnerable families, forced on them by climate change and drought, widespread malnutrition and increasing restrictions on women, since the Taliban overran Kabul in 2021.

events.title: Conflicts command headlines…protecting peace, less so: The UN Peacebuilding Fund explained

events.summary: Libya has been mired in political dysfunction since the collapse of Muammar Gaddafi’s regime in 2011, which shattered State institutions and triggered recurring struggles over legitimacy and power.

events.title: Fermentation, flavour and the future of food: Making sustainability delicious

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Gaza in the spotlight as dire conditions continue
events.summary: The Security Council debated conditions in Gaza at the request of its 10 elected members amid concern that the territory's humanitarian crisis is being overshadowed by wider regional developments. The meeting took place under a ceasefire that has existed in name since October 2025 – but nearly 1,000 Palestinians have been killed, and most Gazans remain displaced. Relief chief Tom Fletcher told ambassadors that “fragile gains” since the truce are “the bare minimum of what Palestinians need.” Full coverage here, and wider Meetings Coverage can be found here.

events.title: Climate shocks accelerating as El Niño threat looms over already vulnerable regions
